output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here’s How to Recover It Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your deposit back.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (And Why They Might Be Wrong)
What a Landlord Can Legally Deduct From Your Security Deposit
Tenant Rights: State Deadlines for Returning Your Deposit
What to Do When Your Landlord Won’t Give Your Deposit Back
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Escalating Your Claim: Small Claims Court and Beyond
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–60 days after move-out.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear; only actual damage beyond ordinary use and unpaid rent are typically valid.
A formal demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recover withheld funds without immediately filing suit.
Documenting move-out condition with photos and receipts strengthens your dispute significantly.
DepositBack enables tenants to generate and send a state-compliant security deposit demand letter in minutes.
output.cta: Don’t let your landlord keep what’s yours. Generate a legally compliant security deposit demand letter in minutes with DepositBack and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlord is withholding, deducting unfairly from, or failing to return a security deposit, and who need clear, actionable steps to recover their money.
